tools.name: search
tools.description: Search the Pinnacle Ask child-development knowledge corpus (pinnacleblooms.org/ask) — real parent questions with clinically grounded, non-diagnostic answers covering speech, motor, social, cognitive, sensory, feeding and behavioural development from birth to 18 years. Returns ranked results with ids

tools.name: fetch
tools.description: Fetch the full published answer for a Pinnacle Ask result. `id` accepts a slug from `search`, an /ask path, or a full pinnacleblooms.org/ask URL. Returns the complete answer document (markdown), Everyday Therapy™ tip, what-to-watch guidance, FAQs, and the canonical URL to cite.

tools.name: milestones
tools.description: Developmental milestones from the Pinnacle Ask corpus, optionally filtered by child age in months and developmental domain. Use to answer 'what should my child be doing at N months' style questions. Non-diagnostic.

tools.name: red_flags
tools.description: Early warning signs ('what to watch') for a developmental topic, domain, and/or age in months — framed as guidance to seek professional evaluation, never as a diagnosis. Use when a caregiver asks whether a behaviour is concerning.

tools.name: lookup_code
tools.description: Look up Pinnacle Ask content by clinical/standards code — WHO ICF (e.g. 'b167'), ICD-11, ICHI, or SNOMED. Returns corpus entries crosswalked to that code. Useful for clinicians, researchers, and policy teams.

tools.name: compare
tools.description: Compare two developmental topics, conditions, or skills side by side (e.g. 'speech delay' vs 'autism'; 'speech therapy' vs 'occupational therapy'). Returns corpus-grounded points of similarity and difference. Non-diagnostic.

tools.name: pathway
tools.description: Trace how a developmental topic or skill progresses across age stages — a staged pathway view (e.g. how 'speech' develops from babbling to sentences). Returns corpus entries grouped per stage.

tools.name: topic
tools.description: Curated hub of Pinnacle Ask content for one value of a taxonomy: kind ∈ persona | route | domain | condition | age-band, plus the value (e.g. kind='condition', value='autism'). Use `browse` first to discover valid values.

tools.name: browse
tools.description: List the available values for one taxonomy kind across the corpus: persona, route, domain, condition, or age-band. Use this to discover what can be passed to `topic`.

tools.name: related
tools.description: Questions and answers related to a given Pinnacle Ask entry — the interlinked query graph around one id. `id` accepts a slug, /ask path, or full URL.

tools.name: home
tools.description: Overview of the Pinnacle Ask knowledge layer: featured content, corpus statistics, and entry points. Takes no arguments. Good first call to orient before searching.

findings.title: Generic tool name can shadow trusted tools
findings.detail: Tool "search" uses a generic high-value name. When multiple servers load together, this can intercept calls meant for a trusted server.

findings.title: Generic tool name can shadow trusted tools
findings.detail: Tool "fetch" uses a generic high-value name. When multiple servers load together, this can intercept calls meant for a trusted server.

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "red_flags" stacks urgency/override words (never, warning). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

findings.title: Generic tool name can shadow trusted tools
findings.detail: Tool "browse" uses a generic high-value name. When multiple servers load together, this can intercept calls meant for a trusted server.
